step_by_step_usage: 1. Fill the water tank with fresh cold water up to the desired cup level (max 12 cups).
2. Place a paper or reusable filter in the filter basket.
3. Add ground coffee — use 1 tablespoon per cup for a standard brew, or 2 tablespoons per cup for stronger coffee.
4. If using the built-in grinder, add whole beans to the bean hopper and select your grind size (coarse, medium, or fine).
5. Slide the filter basket into place until it clicks.
6. Press the Power button. The display will light up.
7. Select your brew strength (Mild, Medium, or Bold) using the Strength button.
8. Enable Eco Mode to reduce energy use by pressing the Eco button (indicator light turns green).
9. Press Brew to start. The machine will beep when brewing is complete.
10. The thermal carafe keeps coffee hot for up to 2 hours without a heating plate.
troubleshooting_tips: Coffee tastes weak: Increase coffee-to-water ratio or select Bold strength.
Machine not brewing: Ensure the water tank is properly seated and the filter basket is locked in place.
Coffee overflows: Do not exceed the MAX water fill line; do not overfill the filter with grounds.
Grinder is noisy but no grounds come out: Check that beans are actually in the hopper; remove any blockage.
Display shows 'CLEAN' light: Run a descale cycle — fill the tank with equal parts white vinegar and water, run a brew cycle without coffee, then run two full cycles with clean water.
Machine shuts off mid-brew: The auto-shutoff triggered; restart from step 6.
Leaking from bottom: Check that the carafe lid is fully closed and the carafe is properly placed on the base.
maintenance_tips: Descale every 1–3 months depending on water hardness — use the vinegar method or a commercial descaler.
Wash the filter basket and carafe with warm soapy water after each use.
Wipe the exterior with a damp cloth — never submerge the machine.
Replace paper filters monthly if using a reusable filter, clean the mesh with a soft brush weekly.
Empty the bean hopper fully every two weeks to prevent oil buildup.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Place the stainless-steel inner pot inside the cooker base — make sure it clicks into position.
2. Add your ingredients and the required liquid (minimum 1 cup of liquid for pressure cooking).
3. Close the lid by aligning the arrows and turning clockwise until it locks.
4. Move the steam release valve to SEALING position for pressure cooking.
5. Plug in and press the mode button: Pressure Cook, Slow Cook, Sauté, Rice, Yogurt, Steam, or Keep Warm.
6. Use the +/- buttons to set cooking time.
7. Press Start. The display will show 'On' while the pot pressurises (takes 5–15 minutes).
8. Once pressurised the timer counts down. A beep signals completion.
9. For Quick Release: carefully turn the steam valve to VENTING — steam will shoot out. Keep hands away.
10. For Natural Release: let the pot sit for 10–30 minutes until the float valve drops on its own.
11. When the float valve drops, it is safe to open the lid by turning counter-clockwise.
troubleshooting_tips: 'Burn' warning on display: The bottom is too dry — add more liquid and deglaze the pot before retrying.
Lid won't close: Check that the sealing ring is seated correctly in the lid groove.
No pressure building: Ensure the steam release valve is on SEALING and the sealing ring is properly in place.
Steam leaking from lid: The sealing ring may be damaged or misaligned — inspect and reseat or replace it.
Float valve won't rise: Not enough liquid, or lid not locked properly.
Food undercooked: Add more time in 5-minute increments and re-pressure.
Inner pot hard to remove: Allow a few minutes to cool; use oven mitts — the pot can get very hot.
maintenance_tips: Remove and wash the sealing ring after every use — it absorbs odours; soak in baking-soda water for strong smells.
Clean the lid's steam release valve and anti-block shield monthly by removing and rinsing under warm water.
Wash the inner pot in the dishwasher or by hand with warm soapy water.
Wipe the heating element (bottom of the base) with a dry cloth — never use water inside the base.
Check the sealing ring for cracks every month; replace every 12–18 months or when damaged.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Place the blender jar on the motor base and turn clockwise to lock it.
2. Add liquid ingredients first (water, milk, juice) to help blending.
3. Add soft ingredients (fruits, yogurt) next, then hard ingredients (ice, frozen fruit) on top.
4. Do not fill above the MAX line (2 litres).
5. Place the lid firmly on the jar; press the center plug in.
6. Plug in the blender.
7. Start on the lowest speed (Speed 1) to prevent splashing.
8. Gradually increase speed as needed. Use the Pulse button for chunky textures.
9. For smoothies, run on High for 30–60 seconds.
10. Unplug before removing the jar. Turn jar counter-clockwise to detach.
troubleshooting_tips: Blender leaks from bottom: Tighten the blade assembly; replace the gasket if worn.
Motor overheats and stops: Allow to cool for 15 minutes before restarting. Do not blend for more than 3 minutes continuously.
Food not blending evenly: Add more liquid; use the tamper tool; ensure you started at low speed.
Lid pops off: Do not overfill. Hold the lid with a folded cloth during blending.
Burning smell: Turn off immediately; motor may be overloaded — reduce load and retry.
Blade won't spin: Check for obstruction; ensure jar is locked properly onto the base.
maintenance_tips: Self-clean after use: fill halfway with warm water and a drop of dish soap, blend on High for 30 seconds, rinse.
Dishwasher safe: jar and lid (top rack only). Blade assembly — hand-wash only.
Never submerge the motor base in water.
Dry all parts completely before reassembling to avoid mold.
Check blade for chips or dullness every 6 months; replace blade assembly annually for heavy use.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Place the air fryer on a flat, heat-resistant surface with at least 10 cm clearance on all sides.
2. Pull out the basket and remove the inner tray.
3. Lightly coat food with oil (optional) for crispier results.
4. Place food in a single layer in the basket — do not overcrowd.
5. Slide the basket back in until it clicks.
6. Set the temperature using the dial or touchscreen (150–200°C for most foods).
7. Set the timer (most foods take 10–25 minutes).
8. Halfway through, pull out the basket and shake or flip the food for even cooking.
9. The unit beeps when the timer ends. Carefully pull out the basket (it will be hot).
10. Let food rest for 2 minutes before serving.
troubleshooting_tips: Food not crispy: Pat food dry before cooking; increase temperature by 10°C; avoid overcrowding.
White smoke: Excess fat dripping onto the heating element — add a tablespoon of water to the bottom of the outer basket.
Unit not turning on: Ensure the basket is fully inserted; check the power connection.
Food burning on the outside but raw inside: Reduce temperature and increase time; cut food into smaller pieces.
Unpleasant smell on first use: This is normal — run an empty cycle at 200°C for 10 minutes to burn off factory coating.
maintenance_tips: Clean the basket and tray after every use with warm soapy water (dishwasher safe).
Wipe the inside of the air fryer with a damp cloth after it cools completely.
Clean the heating element monthly: turn the unit upside down and wipe gently with a damp cloth or soft brush.
Never use metal utensils in the basket — use silicone or wooden tools.
Do not use aerosol cooking sprays — they can damage the non-stick coating.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Attach the stand legs or mount the TV to the wall bracket (VESA 400×200 compatible).
2. Connect the power cable to the back of the TV and plug into a grounded outlet.
3. Press the Power button on the remote or on the back-left side of the TV.
4. Follow the on-screen setup wizard: select language, connect to Wi-Fi, sign into smart TV account.
5. Use the Home button on the remote to access the Smart Hub (apps, streaming services).
6. Connect HDMI devices (cable box, gaming console) to HDMI 1, 2, or 3 ports on the back.
7. Press Source/Input on the remote to switch between HDMI inputs and built-in apps.
8. Adjust picture settings: Home → Settings → Picture → select from Vivid, Standard, Cinema, or Game modes.
9. For voice control, hold the microphone button on the remote and speak your command.
10. Enable Eco Mode under Settings → General → Eco Solution to reduce power consumption.
troubleshooting_tips: No picture, only sound: Check HDMI cable; try a different HDMI port; press Source to select correct input.
No sound: Check volume isn't muted; ensure audio output is set to TV Speaker under Sound settings.
Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting: Move the router closer; forget the network and reconnect; set a static IP.
Apps won't load: Check internet connection; clear app cache under Settings → Support → Device Care → Manage Storage.
Remote not working: Replace batteries; ensure nothing blocks the IR receiver at the bottom of the TV.
Screen flickering: Try a different HDMI cable; disable Energy Saving mode; update firmware.
TV turns off by itself: Eco sleep timer may be enabled — go to Settings → General → System Manager → Time → Off Timer.
maintenance_tips: Clean the screen with a dry microfiber cloth — never use water, glass cleaner, or paper towels.
Dust the vents at the back monthly with a soft dry brush to prevent overheating.
Keep the TV out of direct sunlight to prevent image burn-in on OLED panels.
Update firmware regularly: Settings → Support → Software Update → Update Now.
Use a surge protector to protect from power spikes.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Charge the watch using the magnetic charging cable until the battery icon shows full (approximately 2 hours).
2. Press and hold the side button for 3 seconds to power on.
3. Download the FitTrack app on your phone (iOS or Android) and create an account.
4. On the watch, go to Settings → Bluetooth → Pair. Open the app and tap '+' to add device.
5. Enter your profile details in the app (height, weight, age, gender) for accurate health tracking.
6. Wear the watch on your non-dominant wrist with the back sensor touching skin.
7. Press the side button to cycle through: Watch face → Steps → Heart Rate → Sleep → GPS → Notifications.
8. To start a workout: tap the Workout icon, select activity type (Running, Cycling, Swimming, etc.), tap Start.
9. GPS auto-activates during outdoor workouts — ensure Bluetooth is on for phone GPS pairing.
10. To track sleep, wear the watch to bed — sleep tracking starts automatically.
troubleshooting_tips: Heart rate reading seems inaccurate: Ensure the watch is snug (not too tight or loose) on your wrist; clean the sensor.
GPS not working: Enable phone GPS; go outdoors and wait 30–60 seconds for a signal lock.
Watch not syncing to app: Force-close the app; turn off and on Bluetooth; re-pair if needed.
Screen not responding to touch: Wipe the screen dry; restart by holding the button for 5 seconds.
Battery draining fast: Disable always-on display; reduce GPS tracking frequency; update firmware.
Notifications not appearing on watch: In the phone app, go to Notifications and enable each app individually.
Step count seems off: Recalibrate stride length in the app under Profile → Advanced → Calibrate.
maintenance_tips: Rinse with fresh water after swimming or intense exercise; dry with a soft cloth.
Clean the band weekly with mild soap and water; remove the band fully for deep cleaning.
Charge every 7 days or when battery drops below 20%.
Avoid exposing to extreme temperatures (below -10°C or above 60°C).
Update firmware monthly for the latest health algorithms and bug fixes.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Charge the headphones using the USB-C cable; a full charge takes about 2 hours.
2. Power on by pressing and holding the Power button for 2 seconds until you hear a chime.
3. To pair: hold the Bluetooth button for 3 seconds until the LED flashes blue rapidly.
4. On your phone, go to Bluetooth settings and select 'SoundWave ANC'.
5. Once paired, you'll hear 'Connected' in the headphones.
6. Adjust the volume using the touch-sensitive surface on the right earcup (swipe up/down).
7. Skip tracks by swiping forward/backward on the right earcup.
8. Enable Active Noise Cancellation by pressing the ANC button on the left earcup.
9. Switch to Transparency mode (hear ambient sound) by pressing the ANC button again.
10. For calls, press the multifunction button once to answer; twice to reject.
troubleshooting_tips: No sound or poor audio: Ensure headphones are connected to the right device; re-pair if needed.
ANC not working well: Make sure the earpads are creating a good seal around your ears.
Bluetooth keeps dropping: Keep device within 10 metres; remove obstacles between headphones and phone.
One earcup not working: Reset the headphones by holding Power + ANC buttons for 10 seconds.
Microphone picks up too much background noise: Switch to Voice Isolation mode in your phone's call settings.
Charging not working: Clean the USB-C port with a dry toothbrush; try a different cable.
maintenance_tips: Wipe the earcups and headband with a slightly damp cloth after use.
Store in the included hard case when not in use to prevent damage.
Replace earpads every 12–18 months; they are user-replaceable (twist off).
Avoid exposing to rain or humidity — IPX4 splash resistance is for light moisture only.
Update firmware via the SoundWave app for the latest ANC tuning.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Ensure the AC was professionally installed and the power is connected to a dedicated circuit.
2. Point the remote at the indoor unit and press the Power button.
3. Press Mode to cycle through: Cool → Heat → Fan → Dry → Auto.
4. Use the Temperature +/- buttons to set your desired temperature (16–30°C range).
5. Press Fan Speed to choose: Auto, Low, Medium, or High.
6. For Turbo Cool (maximum cooling), press the Turbo button — auto-disables after 30 minutes.
7. Use the Swing button to set the air direction louver: Auto swing or a fixed angle.
8. Set a Timer: press Timer, set ON and OFF times using the arrows, press Set.
9. Sleep Mode: press Sleep to reduce noise and raise temperature gradually during the night.
10. ECO mode: press ECO to optimise energy use — the AC maintains a comfortable temperature intelligently.
troubleshooting_tips: AC not cooling: Check that the mode is set to Cool (not Fan or Dry); ensure doors and windows are closed; clean the filter.
Water dripping from indoor unit: The drain pipe may be clogged — contact a technician.
Remote not working: Replace batteries; ensure nothing blocks the signal receiver on the unit.
Strange smell when turned on: Clean the filter and run on Fan mode for 30 minutes.
Error code E1 or similar: This indicates a sensor fault — call a certified technician.
AC makes loud noise: Foreign object may be in the unit; fan blade may be loose — switch off and call support.
maintenance_tips: Clean the indoor air filter every 2 weeks during heavy use: remove, rinse under running water, dry completely before reinserting.
Schedule professional deep cleaning of the indoor unit every 6 months.
Keep the outdoor unit clear of leaves, dust, and debris; check monthly.
Run the AC on Fan mode for 30 minutes before shutting it down for the season to dry the interior.
Book an annual service check by a certified CoolBreeze technician.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Load laundry into the drum — do not exceed 7 kg (roughly 1 kg per 5 garments).
2. Pour detergent into the detergent drawer: Main Wash compartment for regular detergent, Pre-Wash for heavily soiled items.
3. Add fabric softener to the softener compartment (do not overfill above the MAX line).
4. Press the Power button.
5. Select your wash program using the dial: Cotton, Synthetic, Wool, Quick Wash (30 min), or Delicate.
6. Select temperature: Cold, 30°C, 40°C, 60°C, or 90°C.
7. Select spin speed if needed (default: 1200 rpm).
8. Press Start. The door will lock.
9. When the cycle ends, the machine beeps and the door unlocks automatically.
10. Remove laundry promptly to prevent wrinkles and odours.
troubleshooting_tips: Machine vibrates excessively: Ensure the machine is level; adjust the feet; do not overload.
Door won't open after cycle: Wait 2–3 minutes for the door lock to release; if stuck, turn off and unplug.
Error code E4: The machine is unbalanced — redistribute the laundry and restart the spin cycle.
Water not draining: Check that the drain hose isn't kinked or clogged; clean the filter (bottom front of the machine).
Detergent residue on clothes: You may be using too much detergent — reduce by 25%.
Bad smell from drum: Run an empty 90°C cotton cycle with no detergent (drum clean cycle) monthly.
maintenance_tips: Clean the detergent drawer monthly — remove and soak in warm water.
Clean the door seal with a damp cloth after each use to prevent mould.
Run a drum clean cycle (90°C, empty) monthly.
Clean the pump filter (bottom-front panel) every 2–3 months to remove debris and prevent blockages.
Leave the door ajar between washes to let the drum dry out.

step_by_step_usage: 1. Before first use, wipe the interior with a damp cloth and run the oven empty at 200°C for 20 minutes to remove factory oils.
2. Position the oven rack in the desired slot (middle for most baking, lower for roasting, upper for grilling).
3. Turn the Function dial to select: Conventional, Fan, Grill, Fan Grill, or Bottom Heat.
4. Turn the Temperature dial to the required temperature (50–250°C).
5. The preheat indicator light will turn off when the oven reaches the set temperature (about 10–15 minutes).
6. Place your food in the oven using the provided baking tray or a compatible oven-safe dish.
7. Set the timer using the Timer dial for the required cooking time.
8. When the timer rings, the oven cuts off automatically (on models with auto-off).
9. Use oven gloves when removing trays — the interior and trays will be very hot.
10. Leave the door slightly ajar after use to let heat escape safely.
troubleshooting_tips: Food not browning on top: Switch to Grill or Fan Grill mode for the last 5 minutes.
Uneven cooking: Use Fan mode for more even heat distribution; rotate the dish halfway through.
Oven takes too long to heat: The door seal may be worn — check and replace if damaged.
Interior light not working: Replace the bulb (unplug first) — use a heat-resistant 40W oven bulb.
Strong smoke on first use: Normal — run empty preheat cycle as described in step 1.
Timer not working: Check batteries if the timer is digital; for mechanical timers, ensure the knob is not stuck.
maintenance_tips: Wipe spills immediately after each use (when cool) to prevent baked-on residue.
Deep clean monthly: remove racks and soak in hot soapy water; use oven cleaner spray on the interior.
Never use abrasive scrubbers on glass door — use a damp cloth and glass cleaner.
Check the door seal for cracks every 6 months; replace to maintain heat efficiency.
Clean the grill element gently with a soft brush when cool.
